reason_for_decision: The article passes because it explains when visible and thermal pairing adds real decision value and when a dual-sensor payload is only an expensive substitute for better system design.
strengths: The article is strong on showing that visible and thermal channels contribute different evidence rather than functioning as interchangeable upgrades.
It gives useful deployment judgment by tying bispectral value to cueing, boresight, framing, and operator workflow instead of to brochure complexity.
The sections on when the pairing may not be worth it keep the article balanced and practically useful for buyer-side decisions.
summary_assessment: This is a strong knowledge-base article. It is clear, technically believable, and helps readers judge dual-sensor payloads by mission role rather than by headline feature count.
